title: Council of Europe Youth Facing Disinformation – Why Journalists Matter 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Journalists, Content Creators & Youth Leaders in Europe
summary: The Council of Europe has officially launched applications for its Youth Facing Disinformation – Why Journalists Matter 2026 initiative, an exciting fully funded opportunity that empowers young people to become champions of trustworthy information, media literacy, democratic participation, and freedom of expression. Taking place on 9–10 November 2026 in Strasbourg, France , this initiative is organized under the Monaco Presidency of the Committee of Ministers of the Council of Europe as part of the Journalists Matter Campaign , in cooperation with the Youth Revitalising Democracy project. Young people across the Council of Europe member states are invited either to participate in the international event or lead youth-driven projects that tackle disinformation, strengthen journalist safety, and promote quality journalism. If you are passionate about journalism, democracy, digital media, fact-checking, civic engagement, or combating misinformation, this is an exceptional opportunity to build your skills, network with leading experts, and contribute to shaping Europe s democratic future. About the Youth Facing Disinformation – Why Journalists Matter Initiative Across today s digital world, young people consume information through social media platforms, search engines, artificial intelligence tools, and algorithm-driven content feeds. While these technologies have made information more accessible than ever before, they have also contributed to an unprecedented rise in: Disinformation Misinformation Information manipulation Deepfakes Online propaganda Digital harassment Attacks on journalists The Council of Europe recognizes that safe journalism and reliable information are fundamental pillars of democracy . When journalists face intimidation, threats, censorship or violence, reliable information becomes harder to access. This creates an environment where false information spreads rapidly, public trust erodes, and democratic participation weakens. To respond to these challenges, the Council of Europe created Youth Facing Disinformation – Why Journalists Matter , placing young people at the center of protecting: Freedom of expression Information integrity Democratic resilience Media freedom Journalism Digital literacy Purpose of the Initiative The initiative seeks to empower young Europeans to become active defenders of trustworthy information by: Strengthening media literacy Encouraging responsible digital citizenship Supporting youth-led innovation Promoting journalist safety Fighting disinformation Encouraging democratic participation Building cross-border collaboration among young people Two Ways to Get Involved The Council of Europe offers two separate opportunities under this initiative. 1. Apply to Participate in the Strasbourg Event Young people may apply to attend the international conference and workshops taking place in Strasbourg. The participation call opens: 6 July – 1 September 2026 Selected participants will automatically attend the two-day programme. 2. Apply for a Youth Project Grant Applicants may also submit proposals for youth-led projects that focus on: Countering disinformation Media literacy Freedom of expression Safety of journalists Youth engagement Applicants interested in funding must submit a separate grant application. If selected for funding, project leaders will automatically be registered to attend the Strasbourg event. What Happens During the Strasbourg Event? The international event will bring together: Young leaders Journalists Media creators Researchers Policymakers Civil society representatives Democracy experts Participants will exchange ideas and develop practical solutions to strengthen trustworthy information across Europe. Event Details Dates 9–10 November 2026 Venue European Youth Centre Strasbourg Palais de l Europe Strasbourg, France Programme Overview Day One: High-Level Conference Held at the Palais de l Europe , participants will engage with leading professionals through discussions on topics including: Disinformation and information manipulation Journalism in the digital age Artificial intelligence and media literacy Freedom of expression Democratic resilience Journalist safety Information integrity This conference provides direct interaction with policymakers, researchers, journalists, digital creators, and media experts. Day Two: Interactive Workshops Hosted at the European Youth Centre Strasbourg , practical workshops will allow participants to: Investigate real information challenges Learn professional fact-checking techniques Practice information verification Understand newsroom decision-making Explore journalism under pressure Improve critical thinking Strengthen digital literacy Develop recommendations for future youth action The workshops emphasize hands-on learning through simulations, collaborative exercises, and peer discussions. Benefits of Participating Successful participants will receive numerous benefits, including: Fully funded participation in Strasbourg International networking opportunities Direct engagement with leading journalists Practical media literacy training Fact-checking and verification skills Understanding of AI and digital media Exposure to European policymakers Collaboration with youth leaders from across Europe Experience in democratic dialogue Opportunities to contribute ideas on freedom of expression Increased visibility within European youth networks Financial Support The Council of Europe will cover: International travel Accommodation Meals throughout the event This allows selected participants to fully focus on learning, collaboration, and networking without financial barriers. Working Languages Activities will be conducted in: English French Interpretation will be available during plenary sessions. Who Can Apply? Applications are open to young people who: Are 18–30 years old Reside in one of the Council of Europe member states Have an interest or experience in one or more of the following fields: Journalism Media Digital content creation Human rights Media literacy Fact-checking Artificial intelligence Information integrity Education Youth work Civic participation Democracy Civil society The Council of Europe particularly encourages applications from: Journalists Journalism students Digital creators Youth leaders Educators Researchers Civil society organizations Youth organizations Why You Should Apply Participants will gain opportunities to: Meet renowned journalists and media professionals. Learn directly from experts working against disinformation. Build practical digital literacy skills. Strengthen fact-checking and verification techniques. Exchange ideas with young leaders across Europe. Expand international professional networks. Help shape future European youth perspectives on journalism and democracy. Contribute to strengthening freedom of expression and trustworthy information. Timeline 30 April – 1 July 2026 Call for youth project proposals. 6 July – 1 September 2026 Applications open for event participation. 15 September 2026 Selection process completed and project mentoring begins. 9–10 November 2026 International conference and workshops held in Strasbourg. 1 July 2027 Deadline for funded projects to complete implementation. November 2027 Selected projects showcased during the Journalists Matter Campaign Closing Conference in the Netherlands. Application Deadline Participation Application Deadline: 1 September 2026 Applicants selected to attend will be notified by 17 September 2026 . APPLY HERE TO PARTICIPATE Why This Initiative Matters Disinformation has become one of the defining challenges of modern democracies. From artificial intelligence-generated misinformation to coordinated online manipulation, today s information ecosystem requires informed citizens who can critically evaluate information and defend freedom of expression. The Council of Europe s Youth Facing Disinformation – Why Journalists Matter initiative equips young people with practical skills, international networks, and meaningful opportunities to contribute to stronger democratic societies. By investing in youth leadership and responsible journalism, the programme supports a new generation capable of protecting information integrity across Europe. Whether you are an aspiring journalist, a digital content creator, an educator, a researcher, or simply someone passionate about democracy and media literacy, this initiative provides an outstanding platform to learn, collaborate, and create lasting impact. Click HERE to learn more about this fully funded opportunity for young journalists, content creators youth leaders in Europe. Conclusion The Council of Europe’s Youth Facing Disinformation – Why Journalists Matter 2026 initiative represents a unique opportunity for young people to actively engage in defending democracy, promoting media freedom, and strengthening information integrity across Europe. Through fully funded participation, practical workshops, networking with leading journalists and policymakers, and opportunities to lead impactful youth projects, participants will gain invaluable knowledge, skills, and international connections. If you are passionate about journalism, digital media, civic engagement, or combating disinformation, this programme offers an excellent platform to contribute to meaningful change while becoming part of a growing European movement dedicated to protecting freedom of expression and trustworthy information. Discover more global Conference , Journalism and European opportunities on OFY. title: UNDP African Youth Co Creators Council 2026: Apply to Shape Youth Centered Solutions Across Africa
summary: The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) has officially opened applications for the African Youth Co Creators Council 2026 , a unique continental leadership opportunity designed to place young Africans at the center of policy, innovation, and sustainable development. The initiative seeks to recruit 11 exceptional young leaders from Africa s five geographical regions and the African diaspora to serve on a two-year advisory council that will directly influence UNDP s youth agenda across the continent. This is an outstanding opportunity for young innovators, community leaders, entrepreneurs, climate advocates, governance experts, peacebuilders, and changemakers who are passionate about creating lasting impact in Africa. Applications are open until 31 July 2026 . About the UNDP African Youth Co Creators Council Africa is the world s youngest continent, with more than 60 percent of its population under the age of 25 . Across the continent, millions of young people are already leading transformative initiatives by: Building successful enterprises Driving community campaigns Developing innovative technologies Advocating for accountability and better governance Creating solutions to local and continental challenges Recognizing the importance of youth leadership, the UNDP Regional Service Centre for Africa (RSCA) is establishing the African Youth Co Creators Council to ensure young people actively shape the design and implementation of development initiatives. The Council will consist of 11 members , representing Africa s five regions and the African diaspora. Members will collaborate closely with UNDP teams located in: Addis Ababa Dakar Nairobi Pretoria They will also engage with UNDP Country Offices operating across 46 African countries . Purpose of the Council The Council will contribute directly to the design, implementation, monitoring, and evaluation of the UNDP Africa Youth Portfolio and broader youth-focused initiatives undertaken by the Regional Service Centre for Africa. Rather than being symbolic representatives, Council members will actively co-create solutions and provide strategic advice that influences UNDP programming throughout Africa. What Council Members Will Do The Council will operate under a Triple A Strategy , consisting of: 1. Advise Members will: Guide the design of UNDP regional projects Contribute to flagship youth initiatives Provide strategic recommendations Support monitoring, evaluation, and learning processes Improve programme implementation 2. Anticipate Council members will: Identify emerging opportunities for young Africans Analyze future trends Highlight risks affecting youth Recommend policy and programme responses Inform evidence-based decision making 3. Amplify Members will: Strengthen their leadership capacity Participate in dedicated learning opportunities Share newly acquired skills within their communities Inspire and mentor other young people Key Responsibilities Selected members will participate in numerous activities throughout their two-year mandate, including: Participating in consultations and policy discussions Identifying youth-led innovations that deserve scaling Documenting successful community solutions Working with UNDP Country Offices Collaborating with UN youth structures Participating in regional dialogues Engaging with development partners Working alongside the African Union Commission Collaborating with Regional Economic Communities Producing a final report highlighting youth priorities and emerging trends across Africa The final report will serve as an important resource for UNDP and development partners when designing future youth programmes. Meeting Format The Council will operate using a hybrid model. Most activities will take place virtually, while several important engagements will be conducted in person, including: Council onboarding Mid-term review Closing ceremony Members are expected to participate in at least 75 percent of Council activities. Benefits of Joining the Council Successful applicants will have the opportunity to: Influence UNDP youth programming across Africa Work directly with UNDP regional teams Collaborate with leading development experts Participate in continental policy discussions Expand their leadership capacity Build professional networks across Africa Connect with UNDP Country Offices in 46 countries Engage with the African Union Commission Collaborate with Regional Economic Communities Help shape the UNDP Africa Youth Portfolio Gain exposure to regional development initiatives Strengthen advocacy and policy engagement skills Participation in the Council is voluntary . However, UNDP will reasonably cover: Travel expenses Accommodation Meals Digital communication costs related to Council activities Eligibility Requirements Applicants must satisfy all of the following requirements: Be an African national Be between 18 and 34 years old at the time of selection Be living in Africa or the African diaspora Demonstrate leadership, innovation, or community engagement experience Be committed to inclusive development and youth empowerment Not have previously served on a UN Youth Advisory Council, Youth Sounding Board, or similar advisory mechanism established by another development partner Be available for regular virtual meetings and periodic in-person engagements Possess strong written and spoken English Serve in a personal and apolitical capacity Applications may be submitted by individuals or by members of youth organizations. However, successful applicants will serve in their personal capacity and will not officially represent their organizations. Areas of Experience Sought UNDP welcomes applicants with experience in one or more of the following fields: Systems thinking Communication Governance Political transitions Peacebuilding Climate action Mental health Gender Green economy Blue economy Orange economy Poverty reduction Digital innovation Diversity and Inclusion UNDP is committed to creating a diverse Council. Selection will prioritize: Gender balance Regional representation Persons with disabilities Young people from rural and remote communities Linguistic diversity Selection Process Applications will undergo several stages. Stage 1 UNDP Country Offices across Africa will compile a longlist of eligible candidates. Stage 2 The Regional Service Centre for Africa will prepare a shortlist that reflects regional diversity and inclusion. Stage 3 Shortlisted applicants will be interviewed by a panel consisting of representatives from: Regional Service Centre for Africa UNDP Country Offices Development partners Candidates will be assessed using a scoring matrix. Stage 4 Top-ranked candidates will receive formal notification of selection. Application Deadline 31 July 2026 Interested applicants are encouraged to apply well before the deadline. How to Apply Applicants should complete the official online application form before 31 July 2026 . APPLY HERE Applications may be submitted in either: English French For additional information or clarification, applicants may contact: Ana Dju Email: ana.dju@undp.org Final Thoughts The UNDP African Youth Co Creators Council offers an exceptional opportunity for young African leaders to influence regional development policies, contribute to innovative youth-centered solutions, and collaborate with one of the world s leading development organizations. By joining the Council, members will not only strengthen their own leadership journey but also help shape the future of youth engagement and sustainable development across Africa. If you are passionate about creating lasting impact, advancing youth empowerment, and contributing to Africa s development agenda, this is an opportunity worth pursuing. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Youth Opportunities on OFY here . title: Monash University PhD Scholarship in Climate Displacement in Australia 2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunity with Annual Tax Free Stipend of AUD 37,145
summary: Climate change is increasingly reshaping communities around the world, forcing millions of people to relocate due to floods, bushfires, droughts, rising sea levels, and other climate related disasters. To better understand these challenges and develop long term policy solutions, Monash University is offering applications for its 2026 PhD Scholarship in Climate Displacement in Australia . This prestigious research scholarship provides an exceptional opportunity for outstanding domestic and international students to undertake a fully funded PhD while contributing to an important Australian Research Council (ARC) funded research project. Successful candidates will receive generous financial support, expert supervision, funding for research activities, and the opportunity to contribute to high impact academic research on climate displacement. The scholarship is hosted at the Clayton Campus in Melbourne, Australia, and applications close on Sunday, 2 August 2026 at 11:55 pm AEST . About the Scholarship The PhD scholarship forms part of the Australian Research Council funded project titled: Addressing Long Term Climate Displacement in Australia. The project is led by Dr. Colette Mortreux from the School of Social Sciences at Monash University. The research seeks to better understand the lived experiences of communities displaced by climate related disasters and identify practical ways to improve Australia s response to long term climate displacement. Rather than focusing only on relocation itself, the project investigates the broader social and economic impacts experienced by affected communities. Research areas include: Community wellbeing Financial security Social participation Policy responses Institutional practices Long term adaptation strategies Climate resilience The successful PhD candidate will contribute to the wider ARC project while also developing an independent doctoral research project aligned with the broader research objectives. Scholarship Benefits Successful applicants will receive a comprehensive funding package that allows them to focus fully on their doctoral research. The scholarship includes: Research Living Allowance valued at AUD 37,145 per year (2026 rate) Tax free annual stipend Annual indexation according to Research Training Program scholarship conditions Tuition fee scholarship Single Overseas Health Cover (OSHC) for successful international applicants Funding support for fieldwork Conference travel support Research travel funding Transcription support Opportunities to publish research Possibility of co authoring academic publications Participation in funded research symposia School engagement activities Opportunities to contribute to future research grant applications Supervision by internationally recognized researchers Integration into an Australian Research Council funded research project Research Focus The successful candidate will primarily use qualitative research methods to investigate climate displacement within Australia. Research topics may include: Experiences of communities displaced by climate related disasters Long term wellbeing following displacement Economic impacts of displacement Social inclusion and community participation Government policy responses Institutional support systems Climate adaptation strategies Future policy recommendations The exact PhD project will be developed collaboratively with the supervisory team. Why Study at Monash University? Monash University is Australia s largest university and consistently ranks among the world s top 100 universities. Students joining this scholarship will benefit from: World class research facilities Internationally recognized supervisors Strong research culture Extensive global partnerships International collaborations across Europe and Asia Diverse School of Social Sciences Expertise in geography, sociology, politics, and sustainability transitions Access to competitive research funding opportunities The successful candidate will be based at the university s Clayton Campus in Melbourne. Eligibility Requirements Applicants should possess an outstanding academic background in Human Geography or another closely related social science discipline. Priority will be given to applicants who have: An Australian Honours degree or an equivalent international qualification A Master s degree with a significant research component First Class Honours (H1) or equivalent Strong research potential Excellent academic performance Applicants must also: Meet Monash University s English language proficiency requirements where applicable Be eligible for admission into a PhD program within the Faculty of Arts Enrol as a full time, on campus doctoral student Not already hold a PhD qualification Expected Start Date The successful applicant is expected to commence the PhD program by: 29 January 2027 Monash University notes that there may be some flexibility regarding the commencement date. Required Application Documents Applicants should prepare the following documents: Cover letter outlining suitability for the scholarship Brief research proposal aligned with the project s aims and objectives Curriculum Vitae (CV) List of publications (if applicable) Conference presentations (if applicable) Relevant work experience Academic transcripts Degree certificates Official grading system documentation (where available) Certified scanned supporting documents English language proficiency test results (if applicable) Contact details of two academic referees Important Pre Application Requirement Before submitting an application, applicants must first discuss their research proposal with Dr. Colette Mortreux from the School of Social Sciences. This discussion is a mandatory step before submitting the scholarship application. How to Apply After discussing your research proposal with Dr. Colette Mortreux: Submit your application through the Monash University My.App portal. Upload all required documents in PDF format. Select Admission Only as the Application Type. Select Yes when asked if applying for scholarship funding. Enter PhD Scholarship in Climate Displacement in Australia under Research Program Funding. Application Deadline Deadline: Sunday, 2 August 2026, 11:55 pm AEST Late applications may not be considered. Interested applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their research proposal and supporting documents as early as possible. APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Scholarships Opportunities on OFY here . The post Monash University PhD Scholarship in Climate Displacement in Australia 2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunity with Annual Tax Free Stipend of AUD 37,145 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Google DeepMind Accelerator AI for the Planet 2026 for Asia Pacific Organizations: Equity Free Support, Google AI Mentorship, Cloud Credits, and Global Exposure
summary: Artificial intelligence is rapidly becoming one of the most powerful tools for solving global environmental challenges. From protecting forests and biodiversity to advancing sustainable agriculture and climate resilience, AI is creating new opportunities to develop innovative solutions that benefit both people and the planet. To accelerate these innovations, Google DeepMind has officially opened applications for the Google DeepMind Accelerator: AI for the Planet 2026 , a three month accelerator program designed specifically for AI focused startups, research teams, and non profit organizations across the Asia Pacific (APAC) region. The accelerator is looking for ambitious organizations that are using frontier artificial intelligence to solve pressing environmental problems. Successful participants will receive technical mentorship from Google experts, access to Google s advanced AI technologies, cloud credits for eligible projects, equity free support, and the opportunity to join Google s global accelerator alumni community. Applications are now open and will close on 26 July 2026 . About the Google DeepMind Accelerator AI for the Planet Program The Google DeepMind Accelerator AI for the Planet is a specialized accelerator created to help promising AI driven organizations move from innovative ideas to scalable environmental solutions. The program aims to support organizations that are leveraging frontier artificial intelligence to address some of the world s most urgent environmental issues. Google DeepMind plans to select 10 to 15 organizations for this highly competitive accelerator. Throughout the three month program, participants will gain direct access to Google s advanced AI ecosystem, including specialized frontier AI models, while working closely with Google engineers, researchers, and industry experts to overcome technical challenges and accelerate product development. Beyond the accelerator, graduating organizations become members of the prestigious Google Accelerator Alumni Network, a global community consisting of more than 2,000 startups and non profit organizations . Program Structure The accelerator is carefully designed to combine intensive in person learning with long term virtual mentorship. 1. Week Long In Person Bootcamp The accelerator begins with a one week immersive bootcamp where participating organizations will: Meet Google mentors and technical experts. Attend keynote sessions. Participate in practical workshops. Receive diagnostic sessions tailored to their projects. Define project goals and implementation roadmaps. Collaborate with fellow innovators across the APAC region. 2. Three Months of Tailored Virtual Support Following the bootcamp, organizations will receive personalized virtual support over three months. Participants will benefit from: Dedicated relationship managers. One on one mentoring. Technical guidance. Product development advice. Business strategy support. Collaboration with Google engineers and AI specialists. Industry expert consultations. 3. Demo Day The program concludes with an in person Demo Day where participating teams will showcase their AI powered environmental solutions. Demo Day provides opportunities to present projects before: Investors. Industry partners. Google teams. Global stakeholders. Potential collaborators. This event helps organizations build visibility and create new partnerships that can accelerate their impact. Who Can Apply? Google DeepMind is seeking organizations headquartered within the Asia Pacific region that are committed to solving environmental challenges through artificial intelligence. Eligible applicants include: Early stage startups. Research teams. Non profit organizations. Areas of Focus Projects should focus on one or more environmental themes, including: Nature conservation. Climate solutions. Sustainable agriculture. Sustainability. Energy. AI enabled nature protection. Forest protection. Other environmental innovation areas using AI. Google encourages applications from organizations working across a wide variety of environmental challenges, provided AI plays a central role in the solution. Eligibility Requirements Applicants should meet the following requirements: Relevant Environmental Focus Projects should tackle important environmental challenges related to: Nature. Climate. Agriculture. Sustainability. Energy. Functional Prototype Organizations should already possess: A functional prototype, or A Minimum Viable Product (MVP). Applicants should also demonstrate: Early validation. Proven traction. AI Centric Solution Artificial intelligence must: Be the core technology powering the current solution, or Form a central component of the future technical roadmap. Google AI Integration Applicants should demonstrate clear potential to integrate Google AI technologies, including: Gemini. Gemma. AlphaEarth. Forestry. Perch. Other specialized Google AI models. Strong Technical Team Organizations should have: An established in house technical team. Expertise in AI. Expertise in machine learning. Leadership Commitment At least two or three senior leaders must participate throughout the accelerator. These may include: Founders. Chief executives. Chief technology officers. Primary technical decision makers. Benefits of the Google DeepMind Accelerator Selected organizations will receive numerous benefits designed to help them scale both technically and commercially. Equity Free Support Participants receive: Equity free participation. Ongoing alumni support after graduation. Advanced Technical Training Google provides personalized training addressing: Technical challenges. Product development. Business growth. Participants also receive invitations to exclusive Google technology bootcamps. Dedicated Mentorship Organizations receive one on one mentoring from: Google teams. AI researchers. Technical experts. Business mentors. Technical Partnership Participants work directly with Google experts to solve complex technical problems while utilizing cutting edge Google DeepMind and Google Research technologies. Google Product Credits Eligible organizations may receive: Google Cloud credits. Google for Startups Cloud credits. Google Cloud for Research credits. Free Cloud TPUs. These benefits are subject to Google s eligibility review and approval. Global Exposure Participants will showcase their innovations before: Investors. Google teams. Industry partners. Global stakeholders. Technology leaders. This exposure can significantly increase opportunities for partnerships, funding, and future collaboration. Google Accelerator Alumni Network Graduates join an international alumni network consisting of more than 2,000 startups and non profits , opening doors to continued mentorship, networking, and collaboration. Program Timeline Application Deadline: 26 July 2026 Bootcamp: 7 to 11 September 2026 (In Person) Tailored Virtual Support: September to December 2026 Demo Day: December 2026 (In Person) Weekly Virtual Open Forums Applicants with questions about the accelerator can participate in Google DeepMind s weekly virtual open forums throughout the application period. These sessions provide an excellent opportunity to learn more about the program, clarify eligibility requirements, and strengthen applications. Why This Accelerator Matters The Google DeepMind Accelerator AI for the Planet offers much more than technical training. It connects environmental innovators with one of the world s leading artificial intelligence organizations, providing access to advanced AI technologies, world class mentorship, global exposure, and a powerful international innovation network. Organizations working at the intersection of AI and environmental sustainability have an exceptional opportunity to refine their technologies, expand their impact, and contribute to solving some of the planet s most pressing challenges. Application Deadline: 26 July 2026 APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Accelerator Opportunities on OFY here . title: AmaliTech Immersive Internship Programme 2026 in Ghana (Paid): Gain Real World Software Engineering Experience with Mentorship and a Stipend
summary: The AmaliTech Immersive Internship Programme (IIP) 2026 is now accepting applications from eligible students who are passionate about building careers in software engineering. This fully paid, six week onsite internship provides aspiring technology professionals with an opportunity to gain practical industry experience, receive mentorship from experienced software engineers, and develop the technical and professional skills needed to succeed in today s competitive technology sector. Unlike traditional internships that often focus on observation, the AmaliTech Immersive Internship Programme places participants in structured software development environments where they contribute to real projects, collaborate with professional development teams, and experience how modern technology companies design, build, and deliver digital solutions. The programme is specifically designed to bridge the gap between classroom learning and professional employment by giving students practical exposure to industry workflows while preparing them for National Service and entry level software engineering careers. Programme Overview The AmaliTech Immersive Internship Programme offers participants an opportunity to experience the realities of working in a professional software development environment. Throughout the six week programme, interns will: Work alongside experienced software professionals. Participate in structured software development activities. Apply classroom knowledge to practical projects. Learn professional software engineering workflows. Receive mentorship from industry experts. Build confidence for future employment opportunities. Develop technical and workplace skills. The internship is fully onsite, allowing participants to collaborate directly with experienced engineers and fellow interns in a professional working environment. Internship Locations Successful applicants will participate onsite at one of AmaliTech s offices located in: Accra Kumasi Takoradi Internship Duration The programme runs for six (6) weeks . Applicants may join one of the following cohorts: Cohort One 24 August – 4 October Cohort Two 14 September – 23 October Internship Specialisations Applicants can choose their preferred area of specialization based on their interests and existing skills. 1. Frontend Engineering Participants interested in Frontend Engineering will learn to: Build responsive web applications. Develop interactive user interfaces. Create engaging user experiences. Work with modern frontend development practices. 2. Backend Engineering Backend Engineering participants will gain experience in: Developing server side applications. Building application logic. Working with databases and backend systems. Supporting scalable software solutions. Benefits of the AmaliTech Immersive Internship Programme Participants selected for the programme will enjoy numerous professional and career development benefits. 1. Paid Internship Interns receive a stipend during the internship period, providing financial support while gaining valuable work experience. 2. Real Industry Experience Participants gain hands on exposure to real software development projects while working in structured professional teams. They will learn: Professional software engineering practices. Team collaboration. Software delivery processes. Industry workflows. Problem solving in real development environments. 3. Professional Mentorship Interns receive continuous guidance from experienced software professionals who help them: Improve technical abilities. Develop workplace professionalism. Receive constructive feedback. Grow throughout the internship. 4. Career Readiness The programme prepares participants for future employment by helping them: Build practical technical skills. Gain workplace confidence. Prepare for National Service. Become competitive for entry level software engineering positions. 5. Certificate of Completion Participants receive a certificate after successfully completing the internship programme. Who Can Apply? Applicants should meet the following eligibility requirements: Be a third year bachelor s student or second year Higher National Diploma (HND) student. Be studying Computer Science, Engineering, Information Technology or related programmes. Have familiarity with programming fundamentals. Existing experience in the selected specialization is considered an advantage. AmaliTech also emphasizes inclusion by welcoming applicants from diverse backgrounds regardless of gender, ability, or personal background. Application Process The application process consists of five stages. Step 1: Submit Your Application Complete the online application through the official application portal. Step 2: Application Review Applications are reviewed to determine eligibility and alignment with programme requirements. Step 3: Proficiency Assessment Eligible applicants receive an assessment via email to evaluate: Problem solving skills Learning potential Step 4: Online Interview Shortlisted candidates attend an online interview to assess their readiness for the internship. Step 5: Offer Successful applicants receive an email with their offer and instructions for the next steps. Why This Internship Matters For many students, moving from university education into professional software engineering can be challenging due to limited practical experience. The AmaliTech Immersive Internship Programme addresses this challenge by providing: Real workplace exposure. Practical software development experience. Direct mentorship from industry experts. Professional networking opportunities. Hands on learning. Confidence for future employment. Participants leave the programme with practical knowledge that complements their academic education and positions them for successful careers in technology. Deadline No application deadline has been specified. Applications are currently open, and interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible. APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Internship Opportunities on OFY here . title: Craig Newmark Philanthropies Funds New York University Cybersecurity Clinic to Protect Nonprofits, Schools, and Community Organizations from Cybercrime
summary: Cybersecurity threats continue to rise worldwide, with nonprofit organizations, schools, community health centers, shelters, legal aid organizations, and small community groups increasingly becoming targets of ransomware, phishing attacks, fraud, identity theft, deepfakes, and other forms of cybercrime. Unfortunately, many of these organizations lack the financial resources and technical expertise needed to defend themselves effectively. To address this growing challenge, Craig Newmark Philanthropies has provided funding to establish the New York University (NYU) Cybersecurity Clinic , a groundbreaking initiative that will provide free cybersecurity support to mission driven organizations across New York City. The clinic will be housed within the NYU Center for Cybersecurity , bringing together experts from the NYU School of Law and the NYU Tandon School of Engineering to deliver practical cybersecurity services while training the next generation of cybersecurity professionals. Addressing the Growing Threat of Cybercrime Cybercrime has evolved dramatically over the past decade. Organizations that provide critical services to communities are increasingly vulnerable to attacks because they often operate with limited budgets and small technology teams. Many nonprofit organizations now face threats such as: Ransomware attacks Phishing scams Social engineering attacks Cloud security vulnerabilities Identity theft AI generated deepfakes Fraud Data breaches Disinformation campaigns These attacks can interrupt essential services, expose sensitive personal information, damage public trust, and create significant financial losses. The newly established NYU Cybersecurity Clinic aims to strengthen the cyber resilience of organizations that otherwise cannot afford professional cybersecurity services. What the NYU Cybersecurity Clinic Will Offer The clinic will provide free, practical cybersecurity assistance to eligible organizations throughout New York City. Its services will include: Cybersecurity risk assessments Cybersecurity awareness and staff training Technology and governance guidance Incident response planning Recovery planning after cyber incidents Cybersecurity best practices implementation Public private information sharing Security resilience planning The clinic will also help organizations strengthen their cybersecurity hygiene by promoting: Multi factor authentication Cloud security practices Phishing prevention Social engineering awareness Risk management strategies It is important to note that while the clinic will provide extensive cybersecurity support, it will not provide legal advice or legal representation . An Interdisciplinary Approach to Cybersecurity One of the unique aspects of the NYU Cybersecurity Clinic is its interdisciplinary model. Rather than relying solely on technical experts, the clinic will combine expertise from multiple disciplines, including: Computer science Engineering Law Public policy Cybersecurity governance Faculty members, fellows, and specially trained students from both the NYU School of Law and the NYU Tandon School of Engineering will work together under faculty supervision to support client organizations. This collaborative model ensures that organizations receive comprehensive guidance addressing both technical and organizational cybersecurity challenges. Training Future Cybersecurity Professionals Beyond serving community organizations, the clinic will also play an important educational role. Students participating in the clinic will gain real world experience by working directly with organizations facing genuine cybersecurity challenges. Through this hands on learning experience, students will develop expertise in: Cyber defense Risk assessment Incident response Cybersecurity governance Technology policy Practical cybersecurity consulting This approach prepares graduates to understand cybersecurity from multiple perspectives rather than focusing only on technical solutions. Built on NYU s Strong Cybersecurity Foundation The NYU Center for Cybersecurity was established in 2015 and has become one of the leading interdisciplinary cybersecurity research centers in the United States. The center brings together more than 50 faculty members specializing in: Cybersecurity Computer science Law Public policy NYU Tandon School of Engineering is also recognized by the United States National Security Agency as a National Center of Academic Excellence in: Cyber Defense Cyber Operations Cyber Research The university already offers interdisciplinary cybersecurity degree programs, including: Master of Science in Cybersecurity Master of Science in Cybersecurity Risk and Strategy The new clinic builds upon this strong academic foundation by applying research and education directly to community service. Joining the Consortium of Cybersecurity Clinics The NYU Cybersecurity Clinic will become part of the Consortium of Cybersecurity Clinics , a national network originally co founded in 2021 by the University of California, Berkeley Center for Long Term Cybersecurity. According to the announcement, the consortium currently includes: 61 cybersecurity clinics 29 United States states Eight countries As of June 2025, consortium members had: Trained 3,766 students Supported 921 client organizations Joining this network will allow NYU to collaborate with other institutions working to improve cybersecurity for vulnerable organizations. Collaboration with Government Partners The clinic also plans to work closely with government agencies to improve cybersecurity coordination. Planned partners include: NYC Cyber Command New York State Chief Cyber Officer Federal law enforcement agencies These collaborations will support: Threat intelligence sharing Coordinated incident response Development of cybersecurity policy frameworks Leadership Perspectives Craig Newmark, founder of Craigslist and Craig Newmark Philanthropies, emphasized that under resourced organizations carry a disproportionate cybersecurity burden despite lacking sufficient resources to defend themselves. He noted that NYU s combination of legal, engineering, and policy expertise positions the university to train future cyber defenders while supporting organizations that need assistance most. Leaders at the NYU Center for Cybersecurity also highlighted that cybersecurity extends beyond technology. Effective cyber defense requires understanding legal responsibilities, organizational governance, policy considerations, and human behavior in addition to technical security measures. Why This Initiative Matters The launch of the NYU Cybersecurity Clinic represents an important investment in strengthening community resilience against cyber threats. Key expected benefits include: Free cybersecurity support for organizations with limited resources. Improved protection against ransomware, fraud, phishing, and cyber attacks. Better preparedness through incident response and recovery planning. Practical training opportunities for future cybersecurity professionals. Stronger collaboration between universities, government agencies, and community organizations. Increased cybersecurity awareness among nonprofits, schools, and community institutions. Enhanced protection of sensitive community data. As cyber threats continue to evolve, initiatives such as the NYU Cybersecurity Clinic demonstrate how universities, philanthropy, and public institutions can collaborate to build stronger digital resilience for organizations serving the public. Deadline: No application deadline has been announced. This is an institutional news announcement rather than an open application opportunity. For more information, visit here . Learn more about important news on OFY here . The post Craig Newmark Philanthropies Funds New York University Cybersecurity Clinic to Protect Nonprofits, Schools, and Community Organizations from Cybercrime appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: PEG Peaceful Classroom Guide Pilot Study 2026: Receive a $575 Stipend, International Collaboration, and Certificate While Advancing Peace Education
summary: Education has the power to shape not only academic success but also peaceful, inclusive, and just societies. The PeacEd Gateway (PEG) Peaceful Classroom Guide Pilot Study 2026 offers educators from around the world a unique opportunity to become part of an international research initiative that empowers young people to become peacebuilders while equipping teachers with innovative classroom practices. The pilot study is organized by PeacEd Gateway (PEG) , an initiative of Education for Global Peace (EGP) , in partnership with Arizona State University (ASU) and the Mary Lou Fulton College for Teaching and Learning Innovation . Selected educators will receive a USD 575 stipend , a Certificate of Participation , access to an innovative peace education curriculum, and the opportunity to collaborate with educators from different countries while contributing to internationally recognized educational research. About PeacEd Gateway (PEG) PeacEd Gateway (PEG) is an initiative of Education for Global Peace that seeks to transform education by ensuring that learners develop the knowledge, attitudes, and practical skills necessary to promote justice and peace within their schools, communities, and the wider world. Rather than viewing education solely as academic instruction, PEG promotes learning that nurtures empathy, collaboration, global citizenship, well-being, and active community engagement. About the Peaceful Classroom Guide The Peaceful Classroom Guide is an English-language curriculum consisting of both a facilitator guide and learner workbook. The curriculum provides both low technology and technology enhanced learning pathways, allowing educators from diverse educational settings to participate. It is divided into three developmental levels: Emerging Level (Primary/Elementary): Ages 7 to 9 Intermediate Level (Lower Secondary): Ages 10 to 13 Advanced Level (Upper Secondary): Ages 14 to 18 Each classroom activity is designed to last approximately 50 minutes , although educators can adapt the duration according to their classroom needs. Curriculum Framework The curriculum aligns with PEG s Peacelearning Framework, focusing on six major pillars: Well Being Relationships and Communication Environment and Nature Diversity and Perspective Global Citizenship Empowerment and Service These themes encourage learners to understand peace beyond the absence of conflict by promoting collaboration, justice, inclusion, and responsible citizenship. What the Curriculum Covers 1. Foundations for the Peaceful Classroom This introductory section contains three activities that help learners understand: What peace looks like What peace sounds like What peace feels like Critical peace education concepts The Peacelearning Framework 2. Co Creating Peace Agreements This section also includes three interactive activities where learners work together to: Build classroom peace agreements Practice shared decision making Strengthen classroom relationships Promote mutual respect Develop a peaceful classroom culture 3. Classroom Connect This is one of the most innovative components of the curriculum. It includes: Five classroom activities Five Classroom Connect sessions One learner survey Learners participate in a glocalized peace and justice project based on Youth Led Participatory Action Research (yPAR) . Students collaborate with classrooms from different parts of the world through: Live virtual sessions Recorded video exchanges Cross cultural discussions Collaborative learning experiences The objective is to help students connect local issues with global movements while strengthening intercultural understanding. Benefits for Educators Selected educators will receive numerous professional development opportunities, including: A USD 575 stipend (minus applicable bank transfer fees) Official Certificate of Participation Complete implementation curriculum guide Opportunity to collaborate with an international triad of educators teaching similar age groups Professional networking with educators worldwide Participation in internationally recognized education research Experience implementing peace education strategies Access to innovative teaching methodologies Opportunity to strengthen cross curricular learning Contribution to improving global education practices Benefits for Students Learners participating in the program will: Develop peacebuilding skills Learn collaborative decision making Explore justice and global citizenship Build critical thinking skills Participate in Youth Led Participatory Action Research Connect with students from different countries Develop intercultural understanding Learn within a psychologically safe classroom environment Build leadership and communication skills Pilot Study Commitments Since this is an approved research study by the Arizona State University Institutional Review Board (IRB) , participating educators will be expected to complete several responsibilities. These include: Curriculum Implementation Teachers will: Deliver the curriculum over approximately 8 to 10 weeks Follow the implementation schedule alongside an assigned international educator triad Communication and Coordination Educators will: Obtain school administrator approval Coordinate parent or guardian consent Collect learner assent Work with the research team where translations are needed Coordinate Classroom Connect activities with international partners Research Participation Teachers will also: Upload required consent documentation Submit classroom artifacts Complete an educator survey (approximately 30 minutes) Administer learner surveys (approximately 30 minutes) Participate in a virtual educator focus group lasting approximately 90 minutes 2026 Pilot Study Timeline The expected schedule includes: Early August 2026: Educator consent and school administrator approval Early to Mid September 2026: Parent or guardian consent and learner assent Mid September or Early October to End of November 2026: Curriculum implementation, classroom artifact collection, and virtual observations where applicable Mid November to Early December 2026: Educator survey, learner survey, and educator focus group Eligibility Requirements Applicants should: Be a teacher or educator. Teach learners aged 7 to 18 years. Use English as the primary classroom language. Have support from school administrators where possible. Be willing to discuss peace, justice, well-being, diversity, and global citizenship. Be willing to implement the curriculum over an 8 to 10 week period. Participate fully in the research process. Coordinate with international educator partners. Have access to Google Drive and video conferencing platforms such as Zoom or Google Meet. Ideally have reliable internet access for Classroom Connect activities. Why This Pilot Study Matters The Peaceful Classroom Guide Pilot Study goes beyond traditional curriculum implementation. It places educators at the center of a global movement dedicated to creating more peaceful classrooms and communities. By participating, educators contribute valuable insights that will help refine an innovative curriculum while providing students with meaningful opportunities to develop leadership, empathy, critical consciousness, and intercultural understanding. The collaboration between Education for Global Peace and Arizona State University also ensures that participants contribute to evidence-based educational research with potential global impact. Application Deadline Applications must be submitted by 11:59 a.m. Pacific Time on July 26, 2026. Successful applicants will be notified shortly after the application review process. APPLY HERE Discover more Global Research Opportunities on OFY here . The post PEG Peaceful Classroom Guide Pilot Study 2026: Receive a $575 Stipend, International Collaboration, and Certificate While Advancing Peace Education appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fully Funded Trinity Research Doctorate Award 2026–2030 in Nature-Based Solutions for Catchment Management in Ireland
summary: Are you passionate about environmental history, climate adaptation, landscape change, GIS, or community-based environmental research? Trinity College Dublin is inviting applications for a fully funded PhD studentship under the prestigious Trinity Research Doctorate Award (TRDA) 2026–2030 . This exciting doctoral opportunity focuses on Nature-Based Solutions for Catchment Management in Ireland and offers scholars the chance to contribute to cutting-edge interdisciplinary climate research while receiving full financial support. The PhD forms part of the ambitious Landscape Living Lab: Catchment Monitoring, Modelling, and Management project, funded through the AIB Trinity Climate Hub Trinity Research Doctorate Award Scheme . The successful candidate will work alongside leading academics and fellow PhD researchers to help shape innovative approaches to climate resilience, flood risk management, and sustainable landscape governance in Ireland. Application Deadline: Monday, 20 July 2026 About the Trinity Research Doctorate Award The Trinity Research Doctorate Award (TRDA) is one of Trinity College Dublin s flagship doctoral funding schemes that supports outstanding interdisciplinary research tackling global challenges. This project is developed under the theme of Nature-Based Solutions , bringing together expertise from environmental humanities, geography, civil engineering, environmental science, business, and climate research. The successful PhD candidate will become part of a collaborative research cohort examining the complex interactions between environmental systems and human societies within a river catchment in County Wicklow, Ireland . About the Landscape Living Lab Project The PhD is one component of the larger interdisciplinary research programme titled: Landscape Living Lab: Catchment Monitoring, Modelling, and Management The project investigates how river catchments can become more resilient to climate change through integrated research across four interconnected research pillars known as the 4Ms : Monitoring Current environmental dynamics Climate-related risks Nature-related risks Modelling Future resilience pathways Climate adaptation scenarios Predictive environmental modelling Memory Historical landscape change Environmental history Community knowledge Historical vulnerability Management Decision-making frameworks Nature-based solutions Sustainable catchment governance Each PhD candidate will specialize in one pillar while collaborating closely with the others. About This PhD Project The advertised studentship focuses on the Memory pillar under the title: Reconstructing Catchment Memory: Environmental History, Landscape Change and Community Knowledge for Nature-Based Catchment Management in an Irish Living Lab The project will be supervised by: Associate Professor Francis Ludlow School of Histories and Humanities Trinity College Dublin Research Objectives The research aims to reconstruct the environmental and social history of a river catchment in County Wicklow. The student will investigate how landscapes have changed over time and how these changes influence today s environmental challenges, particularly flooding and climate resilience. The research combines multiple disciplines including: Environmental history Historical GIS Digital humanities Art history Oral history Landscape studies Climate adaptation Nature-based solutions What Will the PhD Research Involve? The successful researcher will examine a wide variety of historical and environmental sources, including: Historical estate records Historical maps Aerial photography Paintings Photographs Forestry documentation Drainage records Local government archives Rural district council documents Historical newspaper reports Flood records Landscape change documentation These materials will be digitized and integrated into a Geographic Information System (GIS) to reconstruct historical environmental conditions. Community Engagement and Participatory Mapping An important feature of the project is collaboration with local communities. The PhD researcher will work directly with: Local residents Farmers Landholders Community organizations Catchment stakeholders Using ethical participatory methods, the researcher will collect community knowledge about: Historical woodland cover Wetland loss River changes Flood-prone areas Historic drainage systems Field boundaries Land improvement projects Infrastructure development Community mental maps will then be compared with documentary evidence to understand both environmental change and collective memory. Key Research Questions The project seeks answers to several important questions, including: How have land use and land cover changed across the catchment? When did significant environmental changes occur? Which historical management decisions contributed to today s flood risks? How can historical records be integrated into GIS-based environmental analysis? How can oral histories improve understanding of environmental change? How can historical evidence improve future nature-based catchment management? Candidate Profile Applicants should ideally possess: At least an Upper Second-Class Honours Bachelor s degree A Master s degree or equivalent experience is highly desirable Relevant academic backgrounds include: Environmental History Historical GIS Geography Digital Humanities Landscape Studies Archaeology Anthropology Art History Environmental Science Related disciplines Desired Skills and Experience Applicants should demonstrate experience or strong interest in: Historical archive research Documentary analysis Historical map interpretation Visual source analysis GIS software (QGIS, ArcGIS or equivalent) Digital mapping Spatial databases Environmental history Climate adaptation Nature-based solutions Flood risk studies Oral history Community engagement Participatory mapping Qualitative research methods Interdisciplinary research Academic writing Public communication The successful candidate should also be comfortable engaging respectfully with local communities and collaborating across disciplines. Funding Benefits This is a fully funded four-year PhD scholarship that includes: Annual tax-free stipend of €25,000 Full tuition fee coverage Four years of funding Opportunity to work within a major interdisciplinary climate research programme Collaboration with leading researchers at Trinity College Dublin Training in advanced interdisciplinary research methods Participation in the AIB Trinity Climate Hub Application Process Applicants should email their application to: Associate Professor Francis Ludlow Subject Line: Trinity College PhD Application: Catchment Memory Applications should include: Curriculum Vitae (CV) 1–2 page personal statement The personal statement should explain: Why you are interested in this PhD Why you are a strong candidate Relevant experience Research interests Applicants should also include contact information for two referees in their CV. Apply to fludlow@tcd.ie Selection Process Following submission: Applications will be shortlisted. Interviews are expected in late July 2026 . The successful applicant will then submit a formal PhD application through Trinity College Dublin s School of Histories and Humanities. The successful candidate will begin the PhD on 1 September 2026 . Important Dates Application Deadline: Monday, 20 July 2026 Expected Interviews: Late July 2026 Programme Start Date: 1 September 2026 Why You Should Apply This fully funded PhD offers an outstanding opportunity to contribute to internationally significant research addressing climate resilience and sustainable landscape management. By combining environmental history, digital mapping, community engagement, and nature-based solutions, the project will help shape future approaches to flood management and environmental governance in Ireland. Successful applicants will join an interdisciplinary research environment at one of Europe s leading universities while gaining advanced research skills applicable across academia, environmental consultancy, policy development, GIS, and climate adaptation. For more information on this scholarship opportunity visit here For more scholarship opportunities on OFY, visit here The post Fully Funded Trinity Research Doctorate Award 2026–2030 in Nature-Based Solutions for Catchment Management in Ireland appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Reckitt Catalyst Global Accelerator 2026: Funding, Mentorship & Global Support for Health and Hygiene Entrepreneurs
summary: Entrepreneurs developing innovative solutions to improve healthcare, water, sanitation, and hygiene now have an exciting opportunity to accelerate the growth of their ventures through the Reckitt Catalyst Global Accelerator . The globally recognized initiative is currently accepting applications from impact-driven startups operating across selected countries in Africa, Asia, and Latin America. Delivered in partnership with Yunus Social Innovation (YSI) , Health Innovation Exchange (HIEx) , and Acumen America , the Reckitt Catalyst program supports entrepreneurs creating practical, scalable, and community-centered innovations that expand access to health and hygiene for underserved populations. Since its launch, the program has helped more than 80 social innovators across 15 countries , reaching over 2 million people with life-saving health and hygiene solutions. Reckitt has also committed to supporting up to 200 female and underrepresented founders by 2030 , with the ambition of impacting up to 5 million people through improved access to health and hygiene. About the Reckitt Catalyst Global Accelerator Reckitt believes that health begins at home. Through its Stronger Communities initiative, the company invests in entrepreneurs who understand the communities they serve and are building innovative solutions to address pressing health and sanitation challenges. Rather than focusing only on financial support, Reckitt Catalyst provides founders with an ecosystem that combines: Funding Expert mentorship Strategic partnerships Business development support Industry expertise Global visibility Access to networks and investors The program aims to help entrepreneurs scale innovations that strengthen healthcare systems, improve hygiene practices, and increase access to safe water and sanitation. Who Can Apply? Applications are open to entrepreneurs operating in: Nigeria South Africa Kenya India Pakistan Indonesia Mexico Brazil Applications from entrepreneurs in the United States are accepted throughout the year on a rolling basis. The accelerator welcomes ventures developing scalable innovations that improve: Healthcare access Water access Sanitation Hygiene Public health systems Community resilience What the Program Offers Successful entrepreneurs receive comprehensive support designed to help them grow sustainable and scalable businesses. Benefits include: Funding Support Selected ventures receive financial support to expand their impact and accelerate growth. World-Class Mentorship Entrepreneurs gain direct access to experienced business leaders, technical experts, and industry professionals who provide strategic guidance throughout the accelerator. Business Expertise Participants receive practical support in areas such as: Business strategy Product development Market expansion Partnerships Investment readiness Scaling operations Global Network The program connects entrepreneurs with: Investors Government partners Healthcare experts Innovation ecosystems Corporate leaders Fellow entrepreneurs Visibility Participants gain international exposure through Reckitt s global platform, helping increase credibility and open doors to additional partnerships and investment opportunities. Program Partners The accelerator is delivered alongside globally recognized organizations: Yunus Social Innovation (YSI) Health Innovation Exchange (HIEx) Acumen America These partners contribute technical expertise, mentorship, innovation support, and investment readiness resources. Focus Areas The accelerator supports innovations addressing essential health and hygiene challenges, including: Healthcare Solutions improving access to healthcare services and health outcomes. Examples include: Digital health Telemedicine AI-powered healthcare Emergency response Women s health Medical supply chains Pharmacy technology Water Innovations expanding access to clean and safe drinking water. Examples include: Water purification Atmospheric water generation Rainwater harvesting Smart water infrastructure Water treatment technologies Sanitation Solutions improving sanitation infrastructure and public health. Examples include: Wastewater treatment Sanitation technologies Toilets and hygiene systems Circular sanitation models Hygiene Innovations promoting healthier communities through better hygiene practices. Examples include: Hygiene education Behavioral change programs Community hygiene technologies Household hygiene innovations Impact Achieved So Far Reckitt Catalyst has already demonstrated significant global impact. Program achievements include: Supporting more than 80 social innovators Operating across 15 countries Reaching over 2 million people Five-year commitment to support up to 200 female and underrepresented founders Goal of improving health and hygiene access for up to 5 million people by 2030 Entrepreneur-in-Residence Reckitt Catalyst has partnered with Serena Williams as Entrepreneur-in-Residence. As an entrepreneur, investor, and advocate for equity, Serena Williams mentors participating founders while helping amplify their innovations globally. Examples of Supported Ventures The accelerator has already supported numerous impactful enterprises across Africa, Asia, and the Americas. Examples include: Africa Bulamu Bridge AI (Uganda) Débbo Africa Emergency Response Africa (Nigeria) LifeBank Zoie Health Aqua Clara Solutions Iriba Water Group Asia EveryWater (Pakistan) Sehat Kahani Banka BioLoo Solinas Komodo Water Latin America Zócalo Health Isla Urbana O2Eco Global Health Innovation Attane Health Ema ThriveLink The Washing Machine Project These ventures demonstrate the accelerator s commitment to supporting scalable, technology-driven solutions addressing health and hygiene challenges worldwide. Why Apply? Participating entrepreneurs can benefit from: Funding to scale operations Personalized mentorship Global recognition Corporate partnerships Business growth support International networking opportunities Investment readiness Access to Reckitt s global innovation ecosystem Increased visibility among investors and partners Application Information Applications are currently open. Eligible entrepreneurs should submit their applications through the Reckitt Catalyst application portal. Applications from entrepreneurs based in the United States are reviewed on a rolling basis. Applicants should ensure they clearly demonstrate: The health or hygiene challenge they are addressing Their innovative solution Evidence of impact Scalability Community engagement Long-term sustainability Apply Here Why Reckitt Catalyst Stands Out Unlike many startup accelerators, Reckitt Catalyst focuses specifically on community-led innovations that improve health, hygiene, sanitation, and water access. The program combines funding with long-term mentorship, strategic partnerships, and access to a global network, enabling entrepreneurs to scale meaningful solutions that improve lives while building sustainable businesses. Whether developing AI-powered healthcare, clean water technologies, women s health platforms, sanitation systems, or digital public health innovations, entrepreneurs have an opportunity to join a global movement working toward healthier and more resilient communities. title: Allianz Climate Risk Award 2026: Global Opportunity for PhD Candidates and Postdoctoral Researchers Working on Climate Change and Extreme Weather
summary: Climate change continues to reshape economies, societies, and ecosystems around the world, making innovative research on climate resilience more important than ever. To recognize and promote groundbreaking scientific research in this field, Allianz Re has officially launched applications for the Allianz Climate Risk Award 2026 . Celebrating its 10th anniversary , the Allianz Climate Risk Award continues its mission of connecting emerging climate scientists with the global insurance industry, helping translate scientific discoveries into practical solutions that strengthen resilience against climate-related risks. Researchers whose work focuses on climate change, extreme weather events, technological innovation, and resilience are encouraged to apply for this prestigious international award. Application Deadline Deadline: Applications should be submitted before the official closing date specified by Allianz for the 2026 Award cycle. Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible. About the Allianz Climate Risk Award Established in 2017 , the Allianz Climate Risk Award is Allianz Re s flagship annual scientific award dedicated to supporting early-career researchers investigating climate-related risks. The award recognizes outstanding scientific work that helps answer important questions such as: How is climate change intensifying extreme weather events? How can societies better prepare for future climate risks? Which innovative technologies can improve resilience? How can scientific research help governments, insurers, businesses, and communities reduce disaster risks? The award serves as an important bridge between academic research and practical implementation by connecting promising researchers with experts from the insurance and risk management sectors. Celebrating 10 Years of Climate Research Excellence The 2026 edition marks a major milestone—the 10th anniversary of the Allianz Climate Risk Award. Over the past decade, the programme has supported numerous young researchers whose work has contributed significantly to improving understanding of climate risks and resilience worldwide. To expand its impact, the programme now operates across three award tracks : Global Award Thematic Spotlight: Allianz Trade Regional Spotlight: Asia (new for 2026) These tracks allow the programme to recognize excellence across different geographical and thematic areas while maintaining its commitment to advancing climate science. Research Areas Supported Applicants should be conducting research related to one or both of the following areas: 1. Understanding and Mitigating Climate Risks Research that investigates: Climate change impacts Extreme weather events Floods Droughts Hurricanes Heatwaves Wildfires Climate modelling Disaster risk reduction Adaptation strategies Risk assessment 2. Building Climate Resilience Through Technology Research involving: Artificial Intelligence Remote sensing Climate modelling technologies Early warning systems Digital resilience tools Predictive analytics Climate data science Infrastructure resilience Nature-based technological solutions Innovative adaptation technologies Projects that combine scientific excellence with practical application are especially encouraged. Who Can Apply? The Allianz Climate Risk Award welcomes applications from: PhD candidates Postdoctoral researchers Applicants should be conducting research focused on climate risks, climate adaptation, resilience, or technological innovations that address climate-related challenges. The programme is international, welcoming applications from researchers around the world. Why Apply? Successful applicants gain numerous professional advantages, including: International recognition Visibility within the global scientific community Exposure to leading experts in insurance and climate risk Networking opportunities Career advancement Opportunity to showcase innovative research Stronger connections between academia and industry Contribution to global climate resilience efforts Application Requirements Applicants must prepare several documents as part of their submission. These include: Completed application forms Signed documentation Curriculum Vitae (CV) Reference letter Supporting documents Non-technical research essay Non-Technical Research Essay The central part of the application is a concise non-technical essay . Requirements include: Maximum 800 words Clearly explain your research Describe your research objectives Highlight key findings Explain the real-world significance of your work Use language understandable to non-specialists Applicants are encouraged to review previous winning essays published in earlier Allianz Climate Risk Award Compendiums for inspiration. Previous Award Compendiums Applicants can explore previous award-winning research through Allianz s published compendiums, including: Pathways to Resilience: Mitigation and Adaptation in a Changing Climate (2024) Resilience in Action (2023) The Era of Resilience (2022) Confronting Climate Change (2021) A Glimpse of Tomorrow (2020) The End of Normal (2019) Weathering the Storms (2018) The Allianz Climate Risk Research Award (2017) These publications showcase high-quality research and offer valuable guidance for prospective applicants. How to Apply Interested researchers should submit the following by email: Completed application forms Signed documents Non-technical essay CV Reference letter Required supporting documents Applications should be sent to: climaterisk-award@allianz.com Applicants should carefully review the official application guidelines before submitting. Why This Award Matters Climate change remains one of the defining global challenges of the 21st century. Scientific research plays a critical role in helping governments, insurers, businesses, and communities better understand and respond to increasing climate risks. The Allianz Climate Risk Award recognizes researchers who are developing innovative knowledge and practical solutions capable of improving resilience against increasingly frequent and severe weather events. For early-career scientists, it offers an exceptional platform to gain international recognition, engage with leading experts, and contribute to shaping a more resilient future. For more information on this opportunity, visit here Discover more global opportunities on OFY, here The post Allianz Climate Risk Award 2026: Global Opportunity for PhD Candidates and Postdoctoral Researchers Working on Climate Change and Extreme Weather appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
